ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general coffee beverage class (FOODON_03301178). No specific 'iced black Americano' term exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers black coffee broadly. No specific iced Americano sub-code exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 refers to coffee as a substance. No specific Americano preparation code exists; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of iced black Americano is approximately 350 ml (roughly 350 g including ice melt), consisting of one or two shots of espresso diluted with cold water and served over ice.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, coffee, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 171890)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed black coffee used as the closest approved analogue for iced black Americano. An Americano is espresso diluted with water; when served black without sugar or milk, the nutritional profile per 100 g is essentially identical to brewed black coffee. Values are adapted directly from USDA FDC ID 171890 per-100 g figures.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages — black coffee contains no glycemic carbohydrates; GI conventionally assigned as effectively zero (represented as 1 per Veritas convention)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Black coffee and Americano contain negligible carbohydrates (0 g per 100 g per USDA FDC). The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a numeric GI to zero-carbohydrate beverages because GI is undefined when carbohydrate content is zero. Per Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel), gi_category is 'low', and glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. This is consistent with peer-reviewed consensus that black coffee does not raise blood glucose.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (2 mg per 100 g); caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Iced black Americano is very low in sodium and poses minimal dietary sodium concern for hypertensive individuals; however, caffeine can transiently raise blood pressure, so those with poorly controlled hypertension should limit intake to 1–2 cups per day in line with clinical guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; no added sugar; does not raise postprandial blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Iced black Americano contains no sugar or digestible carbohydrates and has a glycemic load of zero per serving, making it a safe beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugar, syrups, or milk.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; moderate habitual coffee consumption associated with neutral to beneficial cardiovascular outcomes in epidemiological literature
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Iced black Americano contains essentially no saturated fat or cholesterol, and moderate consumption (up to 3–4 cups per day) has not been associated with increased cardiovascular risk in major epidemiological studies; individuals with arrhythmias should consult their clinician regarding caffeine intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Black Americano is a cold coffee beverage made by diluting one or two shots of espresso with cold water and serving the mixture over ice, resulting in a long, refreshing drink with a bold coffee flavour and no added milk or sugar. The espresso base is produced by forcing hot pressurised water through finely ground coffee beans, yielding a concentrated shot rich in caffeine and coffee-derived compounds including chlorogenic acids and melanoidins. Dilution with cold water reduces the intensity while preserving the characteristic bitterness and slight acidity of espresso. The drink is served black, meaning it contains no dairy, no sweeteners, and no flavouring additives. Nutritionally, iced black Americano is essentially calorie-free at approximately 1 kcal per 100 g, with negligible protein (0.12 g), negligible fat (0.02 g), and zero digestible carbohydrates, making it one of the lowest-calorie café beverages available. Its glycemic load per serving is zero, which is clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The sodium content is minimal at 2 mg per 100 g, posing no concern for hypertensive individuals from a dietary sodium perspective. Caffeine content in a standard 350 ml serving is approximately 60–120 mg depending on the number of espresso shots and the roast profile used. In Singapore and Southeast Asia, iced Americano is widely available in café chains and independent coffee shops and is popular as a low-calorie alternative to sweetened iced coffee drinks. The dish is tagged under Malaysian cuisine in the source data due to the restaurant context, but the beverage itself is of Italian-American origin and is globally ubiquitous.
common_user_queries: How many calories in an iced black Americano?
Is iced Americano good for diabetics?
Does iced black coffee raise blood sugar?
How much caffeine in an iced Americano?
Can I drink iced Americano if I have high blood pressure?
What is the difference between iced Americano and iced black coffee?

confidence_reason: Nutritional values for iced black Americano are derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed black coffee (FDC ID 171890), which is an approved primary source. An Americano served black without additives is nutritionally equivalent to brewed black coffee per 100 g. The adaptation is transparent and well-documented. GI assignment follows the Veritas zero-carbohydrate beverage convention with University of Sydney GI Database as the reference authority.
notes: 1. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100 g — this is naturally present from trace compounds in coffee; it is not zero. 2. protein_g (0.12 g) and fat_g (0.02 g) are naturally present in trace amounts in brewed coffee per USDA FDC ID 171890; they are not zero. 3. carbohydrate_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 171890; naturally zero as black coffee contains no digestible carbohydrates. 4. sugar_g is 0.0 — naturally zero; no sugar is added and coffee contains no free sugars at detectable levels per USDA source. 5. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 171890; brewed black coffee contains no dietary fibre. 6. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 171890. 7. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 171890. 8. polyunsaturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 171890. 9. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 171890. 10. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 171890. 11. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 171890. 12. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 171890. 13. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 171890. 14. selenium_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 171890. 15. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g per serving is 0. 16. The input cuisine tag 'Malaysian' reflects the restaurant context (The Rainbow Bistro) on field survey data; the beverage itself is of Italian-American origin and is internationally ubiquitous. cuisine field updated to 'International / Café' to reflect the food's true origin. 17. monounsaturated_fat_g value of 0.01 g per 100 g is from USDA FDC ID 171890. 18. All -1 sentinel values have been replaced with actual USDA-reported values where available; no -1 values remain in this record.
